summary: Competitors are leveraging flagship hardware launches and performance-based value propositions to capture market share ahead of upcoming price adjustments. EE is aggressively expanding its content ecosystem through partnerships like DAZN, while Vodafone is directly challenging our speed credentials with its new 'Speed Boost' feature. Sky remains a significant threat in the mobile segment by pairing hardware discounts with highly-rated customer perks.

signals.headline: Speed Boost offers Vodafone customers up to 2x faster speeds
signals.impact: This initiative directly challenges our network speed superiority and could appeal to high-usage customers looking for performance gains without upgrading plans.

signals.headline: Ee Tv Expands Its Sports Entertainment Access With Dazn Launch
signals.impact: The addition of premium sports content strengthens EE’s converged TV offering, increasing competition for our Virgin TV and Stream platforms.

signals.headline: Save £144 on the new Samsung Galaxy S26 series on Sky Mobile
signals.impact: Sky’s aggressive device discounting and 'Best for Perks' positioning pose a risk to our mobile acquisition and retention targets during a major flagship launch window.
